---
title: "Quantitative Trading Project"
author: "Mathew Frame"
format:
  html:
    embed-resources: true
editor: visual
---

```{r}
library(tidyverse)
library(RTL)
library(tidyquant)
library(plotly)
```

```{r}
#as this is the second time doing a QTP, I want to learn from my previous mistakes
  #last time I selected my strategy on what I thought might have a chance to work. This time I want to incorporate clustering and other analytics before selecting a strategy.

#step 1 will be gathering data, and clustering based on useful metrics.

tickers <- c("SPY", "XLB", "XLC", "XLE", "XLF", "XLI", "XLK", "XLP", "XLRE", "XLV", "XLU", "XLY")

data <- tidyquant::tq_get(tickers, from = "2000-01-01")

plot_1 <- data %>% 
  plotly::plot_ly(
    x = ~date,
    y = ~adjusted,
    color = ~symbol,
    type = "scatter",
    mode = "lines"
  )

plot_1
```
